2025-07-24.abstract: The powdered Japanese green tea is increasingly hard to find. Experts say its popularity on the internet is straining Japan’s tea industry.

2025-07-25.title: What Does It Take to Make Your Own Embassy? In India, Not Much.
2025-07-25.abstract: For almost eight years, the police said, an Indian man hid a range of criminal activities behind fake diplomatic missions before finally being caught this week.

2025-07-25.title: Iran Started New Talks Over Its Nuclear Program. Here’s What to Know.
2025-07-25.abstract: Three European powers met with an Iranian minister to try to reopen negotiations over the limits of its nuclear activities.

2025-07-25.title: Thailand Warns of War With Cambodia as Deadly Clashes Enter 2nd Day
2025-07-25.abstract: More than 100,000 people have been displaced in the deadliest conflict between the two nations in 14 years.

2025-07-25.title: Australia Says Gaza Situation Beyond ‘Worst Fears’ as Pressure Mounts on Israel
2025-07-25.abstract: Some of Israel’s closest allies have stepped up criticism of its restrictions on aid to Gaza, where doctors and aid organizations say people are dying of starvation.

2025-07-25.title: War Amputees Find New Purpose on the Golf Course
2025-07-25.abstract: The sport helps Ukrainian soldiers who have lost limbs in the war against Russia to heal and to master their prosthetics, rehabilitation experts say.

2025-07-26.title: Taiwan Recall Campaign Fizzles, in a Setback to Its President
2025-07-26.abstract: An unprecedented vote to remove lawmakers from office could have handed President Lai Ching-te more power by ousting opponents. It didn’t.

2025-07-26.title: Trump’s Trip to Scotland Echoes an Earlier Visit, When He Applauded Brexit
2025-07-26.abstract: Before his 2016 election, President Trump correctly predicted that the forces animating Brexit would go beyond the United Kingdom. But now most Britons say Brexit was a mistake.

2025-07-26.title: Trump Says He Expects Quick Cease-Fire Between Thailand and Cambodia
2025-07-26.abstract: President Trump said he had intervened to help stop the deadly strife, now in its third day, after it extended to provinces about 200 miles south of where clashes began.

2025-07-26.title: Drivers vs. Cyclists: A Battle for the Streets in Canada’s Largest City
2025-07-26.abstract: Cyclists in Toronto are resisting a law that would have the city rip out miles of bike lanes, setting back efforts toward safer streets.

2025-07-26.title: Removal of Castro and Guevara Statues Ignites Outcry in Mexico
2025-07-26.abstract: The Cuban Revolution leaders joined forces in Mexico in 1955. A local mayor removed a memorial to them, drawing protests and condemnation from Mexico’s highest office.

2025-07-27.title: At Trump’s Urging, Thailand and Cambodia to Meet for Cease-Fire Talks
2025-07-27.abstract: After calls by President Trump, the leaders of Cambodia and Thailand are set to meet in Malaysia on Monday to discuss a halt to the fighting.

2025-07-27.title: Dance Poles and Leopard-Print Walls: Love Motels Ready Rooms for Climate Summit
2025-07-27.abstract: The Brazilian city of Belém, host to the 30th U.N. Climate Change Conference, is short on hotel rooms. Love motels are offering a solution.

2025-07-27.title: Israel Intercepts Gaza-Bound Ship of Activists and Aid
2025-07-27.abstract: It was at least the third vessel blocked this year while challenging Israel’s naval blockade and seeking to deliver supplies by sea to a population facing rising starvation.

2025-07-27.title: The Far Right in Germany Wants to Soften Its Image, Not Its Policies
2025-07-27.abstract: The Alternative for Germany, or AfD, has adopted a code of conduct for its members, without changing its positions on immigration and other issues.

2025-07-27.title: Germany’s Far-Right Party May Be Banned. Its Voters Fear Being Left Voiceless.
2025-07-27.abstract: Supporters of the Alternative for Germany say they might quit voting if the party is outlawed. Some opponents are against such a move, too.

2025-07-28.title: Last Soldiers of an Imperial Army Have a Warning for Young Generations
2025-07-28.abstract: As the 80th anniversary of the end of World War II approaches, only a few veterans of Japan’s brutal war remain. “Never die for Emperor or country,” one advised.

2025-07-28.title: Thailand and Cambodia Agree to Halt Fighting That Has Killed Dozens
2025-07-28.abstract: U.S.-backed talks to end the border war, in which militaries have killed dozens of people and displaced hundreds of thousands, began on Monday in Malaysia.

2025-07-28.title: ​North Korea Rejects New South Korean Leader’s Peace Overtures
2025-07-28.abstract: In its first comment on President Lee Jae Myung of South Korea, North Korea dismissed a call for dialogue, dampening hopes of a new chapter in relations.

2025-07-28.title: Europe Accepts a Trump Trade Deal With Other Worries in Mind
2025-07-28.abstract: The framework agreement will likely not do much for economic growth on either side. But it avoids new fissures on other foreign policy issues, particularly the war in Ukraine.

2025-07-28.title: Russia Counters Ukrainian Drones by Turning Off Russians’ Mobile Internet
2025-07-28.abstract: The same data networks that enable phone apps and web surfing help drones navigate, so officials are imposing daily, patchwork shutdowns. The actions can be very disruptive to daily life.

2025-07-29.title: Villagers Trapped as Heavy Rains in Beijing Leave Nearly 40 Dead
2025-07-29.abstract: The rains set off flooding and landslides, forcing tens of thousands of people to flee their homes in the capital and neighboring Hebei Province.

2025-07-29.title: Starmer Is Edging Closer to Recognizing Palestinian Statehood, U.K. Officials Say
2025-07-29.abstract: Two government officials said Britain was actively weighing the recognition of a Palestinian state, in a shift driven by public pressure over starvation in Gaza.

2025-07-29.title: Finland’s Short, Precious Summers Are Plagued by Goose Poop
2025-07-29.abstract: Finns trying to enjoy beaches and parks during their all-too-brief summers have been vexed by legions of geese — and their droppings. The smelly mess has resisted even the most innovative solutions.

2025-07-29.title: Cease-Fire in Deadly Thailand-Cambodia Conflict Gets Armies’ Backing
2025-07-29.abstract: The talks between commanders on both sides were seen as a crucial test of whether the cease-fire deal reached on Monday would result in a lasting peace.

2025-07-29.title: Some Kenyan Runners See Doping as a Path to Glory, and to Basic Sustenance
2025-07-29.abstract: Officials acknowledge that Kenya has a doping problem, but many athletes who are seeking an edge also want a way out of poverty.

2025-07-30.title: Here’s the latest.
2025-07-30.abstract: The 8.7-magnitude earthquake off Russia’s coast led to warnings and advisories for parts of Japan, Hawaii and Alaska.

2025-07-30.title: Wednesday’s Earthquake Could Be One of the Largest on Record
2025-07-30.abstract: It is the most powerful quake since the 2011 earthquake off the coast of Japan that triggered a 50-foot tsunami and the nuclear disaster at Fukushima.

2025-07-30.title: Tsunami Waves Reach California as Officials Warn People to Avoid Beaches
2025-07-30.abstract: Waves were reported along a wide swath of the Northern California coast, including in San Francisco. The rest of the state was under a lesser tsunami advisory.

2025-07-30.title: Here is what the different tsunami alerts mean.
2025-07-30.abstract: Warning, advisory, watch? They’ve got very different meanings.

2025-07-30.title: Thailand and Cambodia Stepped Back From War, but Their Temple Fight Remains
2025-07-30.abstract: Intense nationalism and disputed history — and maps — stoke the neighbors’ hostilities. Even a surge of international diplomacy has brought little hope of a lasting peace.

2025-07-31.title: Thursday Briefing: Trump’s Steep Tariffs on Brazil
Missiles and Drones Hit Kyiv in Deadly Wave of Russian Strikes
2025-07-31.abstract: The assault, which killed at least 16 people in the Ukrainian capital, came soon after President Trump threatened new sanctions against Moscow if the bloodshed didn’t let up.

2025-07-31.title: After Backlash, Ukraine Votes to Restore Watchdogs’ Powers
2025-07-31.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky’s move last week to neuter two anticorruption agencies drew the country’s first street protests since Russia’s invasion.

2025-07-31.title: Aftershocks From 8.8-Magnitude Quake Rattle North Pacific
2025-07-31.abstract: Dozens of small earthquakes were recorded, most off the coast of Russia’s Far East. Five of them have been above magnitude 6.

2025-07-31.title: Myanmar Lifts State of Emergency, Paving the Way for Disputed Vote
2025-07-31.abstract: The military government hopes elections can bring more international legitimacy. But the generals will still rule, and opposition groups are boycotting.
